summary_en: An algorithm that corrects node labels until they give shortest distances from an origin.
A path-building module in the HUD transit battery.
A planning process covering land use and future demand for public and private passenger and goods modes.
The return of results from later forecasting stages to revise earlier assumptions and repeat the procedure.
An empirical travel-time factor representing the effect of spatial separation on trip interchange.
User taxes identified as a funding source for interstate highway construction.

canonical_name: Interzonal Modal Travel Time and Cost

names.text: Interzonal Modal Travel Time and Cost

summary_en: Zone-pair mode-specific travel times and costs used in modal choice.
A zone-to-zone adjustment factor in the BPR gravity model.
The class of urban land use models criticized by Lee.
The network problem of finding minimum-cost routes from an origin.
The housing-market process emulated by the Herbert–Stevens model.
Accessibility by car and public transport used in urban location models.
A resistance term associated with separation or route conditions between zones.
An integrated metropolitan study combining travel surveys, forecasts, assignment and transportation planning.
A zonal total or average used as an explanatory variable in early trip generation models.
A deterministic loading method that assigns each O-D flow to a shortest route.
Traffic assignment applied across a metropolitan-area network rather than a single corridor.
A traffic assignment method that modifies link travel conditions to account for congestion.
A method for mapping densities of straight-line trip paths across an area.
A growth-factor method for adjusting an observed trip table to forecast trip-end totals.
A household interview survey recording residents, vehicles and 24-hour trips.
A method for generating and evaluating internally consistent land use and transportation alternatives.

canonical_name: Integrated Trip Distribution and Traffic Assignment Procedure

names.text: Integrated Trip Distribution and Traffic Assignment Procedure

summary_en: A CATS procedure coupling trip distribution with network assignment.
A sequence of all-or-nothing assignments with updated link speeds.
The iterative procedure used to solve interdependent Lowry Model allocations.
The optimization framework used to formulate the Herbert–Stevens model.
The origin-and-destination factoring method used by DMATS to forecast zonal interchanges.
A survey-based method for documenting movements between origins and destinations.
A staged procedure linking inventories, land-use and activity forecasts, travel estimates, network loading and plan refinement.
The equations used by EMPIRIC to allocate population and employment changes.
Robert Dial's stochastic network loading method for distributing O-D flows over multiple routes.
A CATS assignment procedure that loaded each shortest-route tree after updating link times.
The travel forecasting stage that allocates trips between origin and destination zones.
Methods for forecasting trip productions and attractions from zonal or household characteristics.
An iterative adjustment of interchange volumes toward predicted terminal totals.
The capacity term in the CATS congested travel-time function.
A historical capacity measure defined by acceptable delay, hazard and freedom to maneuver.
An aggregated activity-location model that replaced the RGM in the PJTS program.
The empirical gravity-model implementation documented for BPR computer programs.
A link performance function relating assigned volume and practical capacity to travel time.
The CATS exponential link travel-time function based on volume and design capacity.
A fitted relationship between zonal trip interchanges, trip ends and zone separation.
A regression-based urban development model developed for the Boston Regional Planning Project.
A spatial interaction model used to distribute trips or truck flows.

A linear-programming residential location model designed to emulate spatial market clearing.
A gravity-based model for interzonal truck flows in Toronto.
Modal split calibration and application modules in the HUD transit battery.
A trip distribution model based on ordered destination opportunities and a probability of acceptance.
A two-sector urban activity location model also called Model of Metropolis.
The PJTS model proposed to simulate location, trip making, land markets and transport interactions.
A modal split model applied to trip origins and destinations before distribution.
A modal split model applied to distributed zone-to-zone interchanges.
Contractor for the Urban Mass Transit Planning Project.
Chicago research institute contracted to investigate shortest-route computation for CATS.
U.S. federal road agency that developed travel forecasting methods, manuals and computer programs.
Developer of the EMPIRIC model.

summary_en: Federal department responsible for urban public transit planning during the period described.
Co-formulator of the Herbert–Stevens residential location model.
Researcher identified with the intellectual development of the Regional Growth Model.
Author of Requiem for Large-Scale Models.
Mathematician who devised a label-correcting shortest-route algorithm.
Economist who formulated the Lowry Model.
Study director of DMATS and an early contributor to sequential urban travel forecasting.
Co-formulator of the Herbert–Stevens residential location model.
Contributor to CATS trip distribution and traffic assignment methods.
Researcher who proposed the STOCH network loading method.
Planner who described the integrated approach adopted by PJTS.
Assistant director for research and planning in CATS.
PJTS analyst who outlined the Regional Growth Model.
The Michigan metropolitan area studied by DMATS.
U.S. legislation that designated the interstate highway system.
U.S. legislation that funded construction of the interstate highway system with user taxes.
U.S. legislation requiring a comprehensive urban transportation planning process.
The Chicago survey whose recorded trips were analyzed with the Cartographatron.
The future expressway network plan prepared and iteratively revised by DMATS.
The eastern Massachusetts planning project for which EMPIRIC was developed.
A metropolitan transportation study initiated in the mid-1950s that developed and applied major travel forecasting methods.
A Detroit-area highway planning and urban travel forecasting study initiated in 1953.

A HUD project to develop public-transport planning computer programs.
A first-generation mainframe used for CATS model computation.

A cathode-ray display device used by CATS to visualize recorded trips.

A 1970s transportation planning software system based in part on the HUD transit battery.
